title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: They will renovate the old cinema next year.
‘I'll pay for the coffee,’ said Paul.
I didn't have enough money, so I didn't buy the tickets.
questions.afterGap: money, I would have bought the tickets.
questions.explanation: Tests third conditional 'if' clause (had + past participle).

questions.firstSentence: I'm sure he was very happy to hear the news.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for certain deduction.

questions.firstSentence: I didn't know who he was until he spoke.
questions.afterGap: he spoke that I knew who he was.
questions.explanation: Tests 'It was only when...' cleft sentences.

questions.firstSentence: I find it hard to get used to the noise in the city.
questions.explanation: Tests 'have difficulty (in) doing' combined with 'get used to'.
